jokes.text: It's a whole new beginning. A new day, a new case, a whole new set of lies.
jokes.setup: Lucille tries to be encouraging about the new prosecutor
jokes.punchline: Reveals her expectation that they'll just lie differently
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille character moment - her twisted logic and casual criminality presented as motherly encouragement

jokes.text: Michael was lying. He found his fugitive father earlier in the week, and he was hiding him in the attic
jokes.setup: Michael claims he's not lying anymore
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately contradicts him with major lie
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator subversion - immediately undercutting character claims with omniscient knowledge

jokes.text: Those are the whites only, yes?
jokes.punchline: Phrasing evokes segregation while talking about food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that sounds problematic but is technically innocent - peak AD uncomfortable humor

jokes.text: I'm being lied... on.
jokes.setup: Lucille trying to express that she's a victim
jokes.explanation: Lucille's entitled confusion about grammar fits her character perfectly

jokes.text: Wow, now more is going to come up than go down.
jokes.punchline: Predicting she'll vomit more than she swallows
jokes.explanation: Gross but economical observation about family dysfunction

jokes.text: She's just so... loving and affectionate. I-I... this is not the woman I fell in love with.
jokes.punchline: He's upset that Lucille is being too nice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - most people complain partners aren't affectionate enough, Oscar has opposite problem

jokes.text: This is the nicest she's been since she found out that Rosa could breast-feed Buster.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Lucille is being nice
jokes.punchline: References absurd past plot about adult breastfeeding
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that deepens family dysfunction while explaining current behavior

jokes.text: Aren't you supposed to yell at me and call me 'homo' and motivate me over this wall?
jokes.explanation: Buster's innocent request for abuse shows his warped understanding of motivation

jokes.text: We can't do that anymore. Lawsuit.
jokes.punchline: Political correctness has neutered drill sergeants
jokes.explanation: Sharp two-word punchline about modern military constraints

jokes.text: It was a recent ruling, prosecuted by the same man who Michael was soon to meet with.
jokes.punchline: Connects to main plot with prosecutor
jokes.explanation: Elegant plot connection that shows how everything in AD universe interconnects

jokes.text: It's going to be a long time before Sergeant Wendell Baker calls someone 'Private Homo' again.
jokes.explanation: Absurd military rank/insult combination that sounds like it could be real

jokes.text: God willing, he'll fail boot camp. Well, why wouldn't he? He already failed day camp.
jokes.punchline: Cites day camp failure as precedent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille logic - treating day camp and boot camp as equivalent challenges

jokes.text: Ann got you a B-minus?
jokes.setup: George-Michael mentions getting B-minus despite studying with Ann
jokes.punchline: Michael blames Ann for the grade
jokes.explanation: Michael's immediate assumption that Ann is the problem reveals his bias

jokes.text: Well, it wasn't Ann. She's an expert in math. - Isn't that cute? - Is it?
jokes.punchline: Michael questions if being good at math is cute
jokes.explanation: Michael's flat delivery undermines George-Michael's attempt to praise Ann

jokes.text: Michael felt his son was setting the bar too low with his dating standards.
jokes.setup: Context about Michael's opinion of Ann
jokes.explanation: Narrator directly calling out Michael's shallow parenting

jokes.text: Oh, George-Michael, never settle for fifth choice. Something better is going to come along. She just has to.
jokes.explanation: Michael's transparent attempt to discourage his son's relationship while pretending to give academic advice

jokes.text: You know, she's just so pretty... I get distracted, I guess. - Do you?
jokes.explanation: Michael's flat disbelief perfectly timed

jokes.text: I wish I could draw her nose.
jokes.punchline: Focuses on specific facial feature in weird way
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific artistic desire that sounds more clinical than romantic

jokes.text: Maybe it's your eyes. Maybe you need glasses. For your grades.
jokes.punchline: Michael clarifies glasses are to avoid looking at Ann
jokes.explanation: Michael's transparent attempt to keep George-Michael from looking at Ann

jokes.text: I just found out that my cellular telephone was a lemon. It didn't work.
jokes.punchline: Sets up upcoming misunderstanding about Blue Man Group
jokes.explanation: Setup for later payoff about accidentally photographing himself

jokes.text: Coincidentally, neither do you.
jokes.setup: Tobias says his phone doesn't work
jokes.punchline: Lindsay applies same logic to Tobias
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that parallels broken phone with broken Tobias

jokes.text: Pretty brave card for you to play.
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback suggesting Lindsay is equally dysfunctional

jokes.text: Lindsay had been frequenting singles clubs, also still hoping to make her open marriage work... which it won't.
jokes.punchline: Narrator definitively states marriage will fail
jokes.explanation: Narrator's omniscient doom-saying about relationship

jokes.text: Lindsay, it's not a competition. Of course it is, Michael. That's why they call it 'scoring.'
jokes.setup: Michael says sex isn't a competition
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's literal interpretation of sexual slang

jokes.text: Maybe in the '70s. That many? We've only been doing this for a month.
jokes.punchline: Lindsay thinks he's giving her a number
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's confusion about time reference vs. quantity

jokes.text: - Who is it? - Steve Holt!
jokes.explanation: Classic Steve Holt enthusiasm - always announces himself like he's famous

jokes.text: - Is this your sister? - No... but you just made my day.
jokes.setup: Steve asks if Lindsay is Maeby's sister
jokes.punchline: Maeby is flattered by age confusion
jokes.explanation: Sets up Lindsay being mistaken for peer rather than parent

jokes.text: Who is it? Steve Holt!
jokes.explanation: Classic Steve Holt moment. His overenthusiastic self-promotion is endearingly dumb.

jokes.text: And had been for several years.
jokes.setup: Steve is a senior in high school
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been held back repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan revelation about Steve's academic failures

jokes.text: I can't promise you I won't tell the prosecutor about that.
jokes.punchline: Lindsay threatens to turn in family member
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's casual threat to cooperate with prosecution

jokes.text: Are you wearing my dead wife's maternity clothes?
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific question that raises more questions than it answers

jokes.text: Yeah. It's the only thing I could find. Where are the eggs? Doctor says they're bad for the baby.
jokes.explanation: Oscar fully commits to pregnancy delusion

jokes.text: Do I look like a criminal mastermind to you? How do you get this ham open? I can... I can't get this ham open.
jokes.setup: George Sr. denies being criminal mastermind
jokes.explanation: Perfect juxtaposition of competency claim with incompetence demonstration

jokes.text: Oh. Oh, look, it just peels off. Isn't that clever?
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s amazement at basic packaging design

jokes.text: One or two? Two. No, no, one. Okay, two... Or three? Three. Unless three is too much of an improvement. I'm sorry, is one... is one in the mix still?
jokes.explanation: Perfect George-Michael neurosis - overthinking simple binary choices

jokes.text: The Patriot Act. Read it.
jokes.explanation: Establishes Wayne Jarvis's no-nonsense character while satirizing government overreach

jokes.text: He's twice tried to microwave a Ding-Dong while it was still in its foil... twice. - Twice? - Two times.
jokes.explanation: Redundant emphasis on Gob's repeat stupidity

jokes.text: He knows less than anyone we have ever questioned.
jokes.explanation: Official government assessment of Gob's stupidity

jokes.text: Gob knew too little to know that, however, and his confidence had never been more shaken.
jokes.setup: Gob doesn't know he's considered stupid
jokes.explanation: Clever paradox about Gob's meta-ignorance

jokes.text: Twice? Two times.
jokes.setup: Michael's emphasis on Gob's repeated mistake
jokes.punchline: Prosecutor's unnecessarily clarifying the already clear redundancy
jokes.explanation: Wayne Jarvis's literal-minded clarification of already obvious information. Shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Did you follow any of that? Yeah, he's hinting that he's going to grant me immunity, but only on the condition that I turn in my father.
jokes.setup: Barry asks if Michael understood prosecutor
jokes.punchline: Michael understood perfectly despite Barry's confusion
jokes.explanation: Barry's incompetence contrasted with Michael's clarity

jokes.text: If you know, give me a high five.
jokes.explanation: Barry's inappropriate response to serious situation

jokes.text: If he's got a shot with her, just give me a little tap on the fanny.
jokes.explanation: Barry's creepy suggestion for communication method

jokes.text: I'm a lot more competent than you can imagine. Oh, I forgot to take the tinfoil off, too. I really wanted that.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry moment - confidence claim immediately undercut by failure

jokes.text: And it's so nice not to have to worry about getting pregnant. The doctor said I couldn't be a mother now if I tried. And that was without even interviewing me.
jokes.punchline: Doctor's assessment of her maternal unfitness
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Lucille's awareness of her terrible parenting

jokes.text: Because that's what you are: a hot-tie.
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun combining hot tea and hottie
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad pun that's cringey in Oscar's hippie style

jokes.text: Stop me from pouring this on myself. I can't take this anymore.
jokes.explanation: Oscar's desperate response to unwanted affection

jokes.text: Is that her rape horn? It's more like a starter's pistol.
jokes.punchline: Reframes emergency device as race equipment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor recontextualizing safety device as race signal

jokes.text: Oh, well, I'm much, much, much older than 15.
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's awkward attempt to clarify her age

jokes.text: You made my day again!
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's repeated inappropriate reaction to teenage attention

jokes.text: She's not my mom. But she said you were her daughter. His daughter. That's my dad. That's a dude? And the worst part is he thinks he's passing.
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Lindsay thinks she's convincing as female
jokes.explanation: Escalating gender confusion with perfect final insult about Lindsay's self-awareness

jokes.text: And the worst part is he thinks he's passing.
jokes.setup: Explaining that Tobias is her father
jokes.punchline: Maeby's assessment of Tobias's cross-dressing confidence
jokes.explanation: Maeby's deadpan assessment of her father's delusion. The terminology adds sophistication to the joke.

jokes.text: Like a little girl. In a little dress. Little saddle shoes. Little pigtails.
jokes.explanation: Gob's escalating self-emasculation imagery

jokes.text: That's a breast pump, Dad. Oh, well, I did not use it for that.
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriate use of baby equipment
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable implication about what George Sr. did with breast pump

jokes.text: I was once called the worst audience participant Cirque du Soleil ever had.
jokes.setup: Michael questions if prosecutor is serious
jokes.punchline: Wayne's defense of his seriousness with absurd example
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wayne Jarvis moment - incredibly specific credential for humorlessness. One of the episode's best jokes.

jokes.text: Well, Michael, I did not find their buffoonery amusing. - About my father. - He's guilty, Michael, of medium to heavy treason.
jokes.explanation: Wayne's matter-of-fact delivery of 'medium to heavy treason' like it's a weather forecast

jokes.text: I just, uh, I just want to have sex with you. That's, that's all I'm good for.
jokes.explanation: Oscar's self-deprecating attempt at seduction

jokes.text: You smell like a pine cone. Yeah, that's the weed.
jokes.explanation: Oscar's casual admission about drug use during intimate moment

jokes.text: I'm on mushrooms! I'm on bad mushrooms!
jokes.explanation: Oscar's panicked revelation about drug-induced problems

jokes.text: I'm on mushrooms! I'm on bad mushrooms!
jokes.explanation: Oscar's timing disaster. The specification of 'bad' mushrooms makes it more specific and funnier.

jokes.text: Those bastards. I did not say that.
jokes.setup: News about weapons of mass destruction
jokes.punchline: Buster denies saying something he just said
jokes.explanation: Buster immediately contradicting his own outburst

jokes.text: Climb that wall, homo! Climb it.
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to earlier setup about forbidden military language

jokes.text: Now, when you do this without getting punched, you'll have more fun.
jokes.punchline: References his own violent teaching method
jokes.explanation: Gob's awareness of his own abusive teaching style

jokes.text: Well, the 'B' got you glasses. Maybe this time you'll get a neck brace.
jokes.setup: George-Michael got a C on math test
jokes.explanation: Michael's escalating punishment system for grades

jokes.text: Oh, I told him she was a tranny.
jokes.explanation: Reveals Maeby's strategic lie about her mother's gender

jokes.text: This close, they always look like landscape.
jokes.setup: The revelation that the photos are of male anatomy
jokes.punchline: Wayne Jarvis's matter-of-fact explanation of perspective
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wayne Jarvis delivery of an absurd but logical observation. The matter-of-fact tone makes it hilarious.

jokes.text: Those are balls. What? This close, they always look like landscape. Nope, you're looking at balls.
jokes.punchline: Reveals photos are of Tobias's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to multiple setups - Tobias's phone, satellite photos, WMDs. Peak AD absurdist logic with perfect visual punchline.

jokes.text: I'm on TV.
jokes.setup: His photos are on news as weapons evidence
jokes.punchline: Tobias only cares about being on television
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias reaction - misses embarrassment, focuses on fame

jokes.text: Yeah, I don't think you need glasses, son.
jokes.explanation: Resolution of running gag about fake glasses

jokes.text: Although I'm sorry that you feel you have no love in your life. I don't feel that way anymore. Thanks. It's been a long time since someone stood up for me.
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of genuine father-son connection
jokes.explanation: Rare sincere moment between Michael and George Sr.

jokes.text: Thank God-- my cutoffs! There were cutoffs up here?
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias ending - finding comfort in his signature revealing clothing
